content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

April 5, 1976

Loren E. McMaster
Chief Counsel
California State Employees' Association
1108 O Street
Sacramento CA 95814

A 76-409

Re: Request for Opinion
No. 76-039

Dear Mr. McMaster:

Thank you for your letter of March 17, 1976, requesting
an opinion concerning the annual miniature golf tournament and
dinner sponsored by the California State Employees' Association (CSEA). Because your letter raises no substantial question involving an interpretation of the Political Reform Act,
no formal opinion will be issued. However, I hope the following informal comments will be helpful.

You stated that CSEA plans to hold a golf tournament and
dinner which members of the Legislature, Constitutional officers and CSEA officials will attend. The entire cost of the
event will be paid by CSEA. CSEA lobbyists will take no part
in the arrangements or invitations to the tournament. Based
on these facts, you asked whether the tournament may be held
and whether the CSEA lobbyists may attend.

As you observed in your letter, the Commission has issued
an opinion which involves similar questions. In the opinion
rendered by California Farm Bureau, 1 FPPC Ops. 107 (No. 75-
067, Aug. 7, 1975), the Commission concluded that registered
lobbyists are not prohibited from attending a dinner as long
as they take no part in making arrangements for the dinner.
The facts you set forth in your letter are analogous to the
Farm Bureau opinion and, consequently, both questions you have
posed can be answered in the affirmative. The tournament may
be held and CSEA lobbyists may attend.

If you wish to appeal the denial of your opinion request,
you may do so pursuant to 2 Cal. Adm. Code §18321. If you
have any questions, please contact Natalie West, an attorney
with the Legal Division of the Commission staff.

Sincerely,

Daniel H. Lowenstein
Chairman

DBL:pvp

TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE/ADMINISTRATION (916) 322-5660 • EXECUTIVE/LEGAL 322-5901 • ENFORCEMENT/CONFLICT OF INTEREST 322-6441

CALIFORNIA STATE EMPLOYEES ASSOCIATION
1108 'O' STREET         SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814         PHONE (916) 444-8134

March 17, 1976

Mr. Michael Bennett
Executive Director
Fair Political Practices Commission
925 J Street, Suite 522
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re:     Opinion Request

Dear Mr. Bennett:

The California State Employees' Association (CSEA) requests that
the Commission issue an opinion on the following:

CSEA is the employer of lobbyists. For the past three years
CSEA has held a miniature golf tournament and buffet dinner
to which members of the Legislature, Constitutional officers,
and CSEA officials, were invited. The CSEA lobbyists and the
CSEA employees acted as official hosts. In the first two
tournaments the entire cost of the tournament was borne by
CSEA. Arrangements were made by the CSEA lobbyists. Last
year the tournament was modified in an effort to comply with
Proposition 9. The lobbyists made all of the arrangements for
the tournament and dinner, but a fee of $10.00 was charged all
participants, including members of the Legislature and Constitutional
officers, in order to make the cost borne by CSEA come within the
$10.00 limitation on gifts made or arranged by lobbyists.

This year CSEA proposes to hold the tournament and bear the total
expense, which is estimated to be $20.00 per person. All
arrangements will be made by CSEA employees who are not
lobbyists and who do not work in CSEA's Legislative Division.
The lobbyists will take no part in the arrangements or invitations.
They will, however, be in attendance.

The following questions are presented for your analysis and response.

1.     May CSEA hold the miniature golf tournament this year
and bear the total expense so long as the CSEA lobbyists take
no part in the arrangements or invitations and all required reports
of an employer of a lobbyist are filed?

Mr. Michael Bennett
Fair Political Practices Commission
Re: Opinion Request
March 17, 1976
Page Two

2. May the CSIA lobbyists attend the tournament?

I note that the Commission has issued an opinion to the Farm Bureau
Federation which appears to involve a virtually identical situation (No. 75-067,
11 FPC Opinions - 107 (1975)). If this opinion is still valid, it would
appear that the above questions would be answered in the affirmative.

Thank you for your assistance. Since arrangements must be begun in two
weeks, a speedy reply would be appreciated.

Very truly yours,

[signature]

LOREN E. McMASTER
Chief Counsel

LEM/cin

cc: Lyle Egan, CSIA

The image appears to be almost entirely blank or extremely faded, with only a few very faint marks visible at the top and bottom of the page. The content is not legible enough to transcribe accurately.
